symbols: multiple figures gathered — Jesse's family assembled as Samuel seeks the one God has chosen
young figure set apart — David, the youngest son, unexpectedly chosen over his elder brothers
pastoral landscape — David's setting as a shepherd before becoming king
description: A faded preparatory sketch or study in warm yellow-gold tones showing multiple figures in an outdoor setting. The upper portion shows a group of figures gathered, with one figure appearing to gesture or pour something. The lower portion shows additional figures in a landscape with trees and foliage. The sketch is loose and unfinished, with some figures more developed than others. The palette is predominantly warm yellows, reds, and greens on an aged paper or canvas.
theological_description: The calling of David (1SA.16.1-13) is one of the most decisive moments in Israel's history. God sends Samuel to Jesse's house in Bethlehem to anoint the next king, but when the impressive elder sons pass before him, God rejects each one: 'The LORD sees not as man sees: man looks on the outward appearance, but the LORD looks on the heart' (1SA.16.7). David, the youngest, is tending sheep and must be summoned — he was so insignificant that Jesse did not even think to present him. Yet when David arrives, God says: 'Arise, anoint him, for this is he' (1SA.16.12). The Spirit of the LORD rushed upon David from that day forward (1SA.16.13). This narrative establishes the pattern of God choosing the unlikely — a theme that runs from Jacob over Esau, to Moses the stammerer, to Mary the humble handmaid. David's anointing as shepherd-king prefigures Christ, 'the son of David' (MAT.1.1), who is both the Good Shepherd (JHN.10.11) and the King of kings.

scripture_refs.reason: Samuel's anointing of David, the direct subject of this painting
The LORD looks on the heart, not the outward appearance — the key verse
Arise, anoint him — the Spirit rushing upon David from that day
Jesus as the son of David, the messianic line inaugurated at this anointing
The Good Shepherd, prefigured by David the shepherd-king
